name: abab6.5 / abab6.5s
tagline: MiniMax's 2024 flagship: a trillion-parameter MoE chat model with a 200K-token context window.
seoTitle: MiniMax abab6.5 & abab6.5s — 200K MoE Chat LLM
metaDescription: MiniMax abab6.5 and abab6.5s, launched April 17, 2024: a trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts chat model with a 200K-token context window plus a faster abab6.…
note: Trillion-param MoE; 200K context; faster abab6.5s variant
overview: abab6.5 and abab6.5s are MiniMax's April 2024 flagship large language models, officially launched on April 17, 2024. abab6.5 is a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) chat model that MiniMax describes as having roughly a trillion total parameters and supporting a 200,000-token context window. It was positioned as a general-purpose model for Chinese and multilingual chat, reasoning, and long-document tasks, served through MiniMax's API as abab6.5-chat.

abab6.5s is the efficiency-focused sibling. MiniMax says it is trained with the same techniques and data as abab6.5 but is more efficient, capable of processing close to 30,000 words of text in about one second, while keeping the same 200,000-token context window. In practice the abab6.5 family exposed a combined input-plus-output limit of 245,760 tokens, and the pair let developers choose between the larger abab6.5 for maximum quality and abab6.5s for low-latency, high-throughput workloads.

MiniMax marketed the series on cost advantages, an industry-leading context length, and fast speed, and said abab6.5 approached the leading models of its day in core-capability tests. The abab6.5 series was MiniMax's last major abab-branded generation before the company pivoted to its MiniMax-01 long-context line in January 2025 and later the open-weight M-series. The abab models were API-only rather than open weights and have since been retired, so treat this page as a historical record of the legacy abab line.
strengths: 200,000-token context window, large for its April 2024 release window
Mixture-of-Experts architecture with roughly a trillion total parameters
abab6.5s variant tuned for high speed and high throughput (~30,000 words/second)
Strong Chinese-language and multilingual chat capability
useCases: Long-document analysis and summarization within a 200K context
High-throughput, low-latency chat workloads via the faster abab6.5s
General reasoning and writing tasks through the MiniMax API

faq.q: When were abab6.5 and abab6.5s released?
faq.a: MiniMax officially launched the abab6.5 series on April 17, 2024. The series included two models: abab6.5 and the faster abab6.5s, both built on a Mixture-of-Experts architecture.

faq.q: What is the difference between abab6.5 and abab6.5s?
faq.a: abab6.5 is the larger, trillion-parameter model. abab6.5s is trained with the same techniques and data but optimized for efficiency and speed; MiniMax says it can process close to 30,000 words of text in about one second. Both share the same 200,000-token context window.

faq.q: How large is the context window?
faq.a: Both abab6.5 and abab6.5s support a 200,000-token context window, with a combined input-plus-output limit of 245,760 tokens. This was a long context for chat models at their April 2024 release.

faq.q: Are the abab6.5 models open weights?
faq.a: No. The abab6.5 series was a proprietary, API-only family served through MiniMax's platform, not released as downloadable weights. The line has since been superseded by MiniMax's MiniMax-01 and open-weight M-series models.
